          </div>\n670\t        </div>\n671\t    </div>\n672\t\n673\t    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n674\t         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n675\t    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n676\t    <script>\n677\t        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n678\t        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);\n679\t        const sessionId = urlParams.get('session_id') || generateId();\n680\t        const surfaceType = 'canvas';\n681\t\n682\t        let surfaceId = null;\n683\t        let eventSource = null;\n684\t\n685\t        // Track pending placeholders so we can find/replace them later.\n686\t        // Key: pendingId (utterance_id), Value: placeholder HTMLElement.\n687\t        var pendingPlaceholders = {};\n688\t\n689\t        // Update URL with session_id for reload persistence\n690\t        if (!urlParams.get('session_id')) {\n691\t            urlParams.set('session_id', sessionId);\n692\t            window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n693\t        }\n694\t\n695\t        function generateId() {\n696\t            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n697\t        }\n698\t\n699\t        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n700\t        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n701\t        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n702\t        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n703\t\n704\t        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n705\t            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n706\t            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n707\t\n708\t            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n709\t            statusText.textContent = text;\n710\t        }\n711\t\n712\t        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n713\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n714\t            const parts = [];\n715\t\n716\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n717\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n718\t            }\n719\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n720\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n721\t            }\n722\t            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n723\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n724\t            }\n725\t\n726\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n727\t                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n728\t                el.classList.add('show');\n729\t            } else {\n730\t                el.classList.remove('show');\n731\t            }\n732\t        }\n733\t\n734\t        async function registerSurface() {\n735\t            try {\n736\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n737\t                    method: 'POST',\n738\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n739\t                    body: JSON.stringify({\n740\t                        session_id: sessionId,\n741\t                        surface_type: surfaceType\n742\t                    })\n743\t                });\n744\t\n745\t                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n746\t\n747\t                const data = await response.json();\n748\t                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n749\t                return surfaceId;\n750\t            } catch (error) {\n751\t                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n752\t                throw error;\n753\t            }\n754\t        }\n755\t\n756\t        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n757\t        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n758\t        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n759\t        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n760\t        let welcomeProjects = null;\n761\t\n762\t        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n763\t            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n764\t            try {\n765\t                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n766\t                if (resp.ok) {\n767\t                    const data = await resp.json();\n768\t                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n769\t                    return welcomeProjects;\n770\t                }\n771\t            } catch (error) {\n772\t                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n773\t            }\n774\t            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n775\t            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n776\t            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n777\t            welcomeProjects = [];\n778\t            return welcomeProjects;\n779\t        }\n780\t\n781\t        async function loadTopics() {\n782\t            try {\n783\t                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n784\t                if (!response.ok) return;\n785\t\n786\t                const data = await response.json();\n787\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n788\t                const cards = data.cards || [];\n789\t\n790\t                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n791\t                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n792\t                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n793\t                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n794\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n795\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n796\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n797\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n798\t                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n799\t\n800\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n801\t                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n802\t }\n808\t\n809\t        function connectSSE() {\n810\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n811\t\n812\t            const url = };\n819\t\n820\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n821\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n822\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', split\n846\t                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n847\t                const createdAt = placeholder\n848\t                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n849\t                    : Date.now();\n850\t\n851\t                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n852\t                const threadCards = placeholder.parentNode) {\n857\t                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n858\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n859\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n860\t                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n861\t                } else {\n862\t                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n863\t                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n864\t                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n865\t                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n866\t                }\n867\t\n868\t                // No intent_id\n882\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n883\t                if (threadCard && eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n899\t                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n900\t                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n901\t                loadTopics();\n902\t            });\n903\t\n904\t            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n905\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n906\t }\n911\t\n912\t        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n913\t        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n914\t        // and apply aged treatment if they've textarea.value = '';\n966\t            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n967\t            btnSend.disabled = true;\n968\t\n969\t            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. 8);\n972\t            var createdAt = Date.now();\n973\t\n974\t            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n975\t            // so a hung server still leaves a container.insertBefore(placeholder, container.firstChild);\n986\t\n987\t            // Track it so we can find/replace it later\n988\t            pendingPlaceholders[pendingId] = placeholder;\n989\t\n990\t            try {\n991\t                var resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n992\t                    method: 'POST',\n993\t                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n994\t                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId, pending_id: pendingId }),\n995\t                });\n996\t\n997\t                if (!resp.ok) {\n998\t                    var errText = await resp.text();\n999\t                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n1000\t                    // Try to parse as JSON for structured error\n1001\t                    var errData;\n1002\t                    try { errData = JSON.parse(errText); } catch (e) {}\n1003\t                    if (errData && errData.error_type) {\n1004\t                        // Replace placeholder with error card\n1005\t                        var errorCard = createErrorCard(errData);\n1006\t                        if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n1007\t                            placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(errorCard, placeholder);\n1008\t                        }\n1009\t             // Swap to the server's utterance_id for consistency\n1016\t                var utteranceId = data.utterance_id || pendingId;\n1017\t                placeholder.dataset.pendingId = utteranceId;\n1018\t\n1019\t                // Move the placeholder tracking to the new key\n1020\t                delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n1021\t {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n                const utterance = data.utterance;\n\n                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n\n                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                const createdAt = placeholder\n                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n                    : Date.now();\n\n                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                } else {\n                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n                }\n\n                // No longer track this placeholder\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n            });\n\n            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n\n                if (!intentId) return;\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n setPendingProgress in Node for tests)\n                    if (typeof _setProgress === 'function') {\n                        _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n                    }\n                }\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n        // Every second, {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_21f4a8e23cac45aa8245de0c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n66\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n67\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n68\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n69\t\n70\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n71\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n72\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n73\t\n74\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n75\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n76\t\n77\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n78\t\n79\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n80\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n81\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n82\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n83\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n84\t\n85\t    let html = `\n86\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n87\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n88\t            <div>\n89\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n90\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n91\t            </div>\n92\t        </div>\n93\t    `;\n94\t\n95\t    // Add latest result if available\n96\t    if (latestResult) {\n97\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n98\t        html += `\n99\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n100\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n101\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n102\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n103\t                </div>\n104\t        `;\n105\t\n106\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n107\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n108\t        }\n109\t\n110\t        html += `</div>`;\n111\t    }\n112\t\n113\t    // Add staleness indicator\n114\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n115\t    html += `\n116\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n117\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n118\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n119\t        </div>\n120\t    `;\n121\t\n122\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n123\t    return card;\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n127\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n128\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n129\t\n130\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n131\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n132\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n133\t    return div.innerHTML;\n134\t}\n135\t\n136\t/**\n137\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n138\t *\n139\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n140\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n141\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n142\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n143\t */\n144\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n145\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n146\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n147\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n148\t        if (c == null) return;\n149\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n150\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n151\t        } else {\n152\t            node.appendChild(c);\n153\t        }\n154\t    });\n155\t    return node;\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n159\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n160\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n161\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n162\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n163\t    const rem = s % 60;\n164\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n168\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n169\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n170\t\n171\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n172\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n173\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n174\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n175\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n176\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n177\t];\n178\t\n179\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n180\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n181\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n182\t// projects' supported intents\").\n183\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n184\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n185\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n186\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n187\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n188\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n189\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n190\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n191\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n192\t};\n193\t\n194\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n195\t    const out = [];\n196\t    const seen = {};\n197\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n198\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n199\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n200\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n201\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n202\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n203\t        return ad - bd;\n204\t    });\n205\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n206\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n207\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n208\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n209\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n210\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n211\t            seen[intent] = true;\n212\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n213\t        });\n214\t    });\n215\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n219\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n220\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n221\t\n222\t/**\n223\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n224\t *\n225\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n226\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n227\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n228\t *\n229\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n230\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n231\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n232\t */\n233\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n234\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n235\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n236\t\n237\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n238\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n239\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n240\t    ]));\n241\t\n242\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n243\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n244\t    ]));\n245\t\n246\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n247\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n248\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n249\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n250\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n251\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n252\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n253\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n254\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n255\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n256\t        list.appendChild(li);\n257\t    });\n258\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n259\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n260\t    }\n261\t    card.appendChild(list);\n262\t\n263\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n264\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n265\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n266\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n267\t    });\n268\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n269\t\n270\t    return card;\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n274\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n275\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n276\t\n277\t/**\n278\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n279\t *\n280\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n281\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n282\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n283\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n284\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n285\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n286\t *\n287\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n288\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n289\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n290\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n291\t *\n292\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n293\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n294\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n295\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n296\t */\n297\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n298\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n299\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n300\t    }\n301\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n305\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n306\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n307\t\n308\t/**\n309\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n310\t *\n311\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n312\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n313\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n314\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n315\t *\n316\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n317\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n318\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n319\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n320\t */\n321\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n322\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n323\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n324\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n325\t    return card;\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t/**\n329\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n330\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n331\t *\n332\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n333\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n334\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n335\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n336\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n337\t */\n338\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n339\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n340\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n341\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n342\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n343\t    return card;\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t/**\n347\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n348\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n349\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n350\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n351\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n352\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n353\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n354\t *\n355\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n356\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n357\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n358\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n359\t *\n360\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n361\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n362\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n363\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n364\t */\n365\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n366\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n367\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n368\t    });\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n372\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n373\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n374\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n375\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n376\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n377\t\n378\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n379\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n380\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n381\t    ]));\n382\t\n383\t    if (utterance) {\n384\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n385\t    }\n386\t\n387\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n388\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n389\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n390\t\n391\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n392\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n393\t    ]);\n394\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n395\t\n396\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n397\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n398\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n399\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n400\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n401\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n402\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n403\t\n404\t    return card;\n405\t}\n406\t\n407\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n408\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n409\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n410\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n411\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n412\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n413\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n414\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n415\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t/**\n419\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n420\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n421\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n422\t */\n423\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n424\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n425\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n426\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n427\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n428\t    return elapsedMs;\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t/**\n432\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n433\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n434\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n435\t */\n436\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n437\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n438\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n439\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n440\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n441\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n442\t    return aged;\n443\t}\n444\t\n445\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n446\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n447\t//     from SSE error events\n448\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n449\t\n450\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n451\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n452\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n453\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n454\t    router_unavailable: {\n455\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n456\t        icon: '🔌',\n457\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n458\t    },\n459\t    all_sources_failed: {\n460\t        title: 'No data available',\n461\t        icon: '📭',\n462\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n463\t    },\n464\t    synthesis_failed: {\n465\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n466\t        icon: '⚠️',\n467\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n468\t    },\n469\t    malformed_router_output: {\n470\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n471\t        icon: '🧩',\n472\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n473\t    },\n474\t    no_match: {\n475\t        title: 'No matching project',\n476\t        icon: '❓',\n477\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n478\t    }\n479\t};\n480\t\n481\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n482\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n483\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n484\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n485\t}\n486\t\n487\t/**\n488\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n489\t *\n490\t * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n491\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n492\t *\n493\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n494\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n495\t */\n496\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n497\t    data = data || {};\n498\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n499\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n500\t\n501\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n502\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n503\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n504\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n505\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n506\t\n507\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n508\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n509\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n510\t    ]));\n511\t\n512\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n513\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n514\t\n515\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n516\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n517\t    if (data.utterance) {\n518\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n519\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n520\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n521\t        ]));\n522\t    }\n523\t\n524\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n525\t    const sources = data.sources;\n526\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n527\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n528\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n529\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n530\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n531\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n532\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n533\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n534\t            ]));\n535\t        });\n536\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n537\t    }\n538\t\n539\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n540\t    if (data.data != null) {\n541\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n542\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n543\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n544\t    }\n545\t\n546\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n547\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n548\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n549\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n550\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n551\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n552\t        });\n553\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n554\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n555\t            ul\n556\t        ]));\n557\t    }\n558\t\n559\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n560\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n561\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n562\t        : 'Retry';\n563\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n564\t\n565\t    return card;\n566\t}\n567\t\n568\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n569\t// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n570\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n571\t\n572\t/**\n573\t * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n574\t * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n575\t * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n576\t * rendering escaping contract.\n577\t *\n578\t * @param {object} result  {summary, data, urgency}\n579\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n580\t */\n581\tfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n582\t    result = result || {};\n583\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n584\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n585\t\n586\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n587\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n588\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n589\t    ]));\n590\t\n591\t    if (result.summary) {\n592\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n593\t    }\n594\t\n595\t    const data = result.data;\n596\t    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n597\t    if (rows.length) {\n598\t        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n599\t        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n600\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n601\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n602\t        });\n603\t        card.appendChild(grid);\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    if (result.urgency) {\n607\t        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    return card;\n611\t}\n612\t\n613\t// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\n614\tfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n615\t    if (data == null) return [];\n616\t    let obj = data;\n617\t    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n618\t        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n619\t    }\n620\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n621\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n622\t    }\n623\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n624\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n625\t    }\n626\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n630\t    if (v == null) return '';\n631\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n632\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n633\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n634\t}\n635\t\n636\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n637\t// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\n638\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n639\t    module.exports = {\n640\t        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n641\t        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n642\t        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n643\t        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n644\t        // Built-in card families + helpers\n645\t        el: el,\n646\t        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n647\t        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n648\t        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n649\t        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n650\t        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n651\t        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n652\t        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n653\t        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n654\t        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n655\t        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n656\t        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n657\t        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n658\t        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n659\t        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n660\t        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n661\t    };\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\n665\tif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n666\t    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n667\t    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n668\t    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n669\t}\n670\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"00bf6283-86b8-48a9-8e36-3327fdd0648d","uuid":"b4100e74-1015-4789-8c7e-9cee610eca99","timestamp":"2026-07-23T03:57:16.756Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/canvas/canvas.js","content":"/*\n * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {object} result  {summary, data, urgency}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n    module.exports = {\n        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n        // Built-in card families + helpers\n        el: el,\n        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n    };\n}\n\n// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\nif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n}\n","numLines":670,"startLine":1,"totalLines":670}}}
