{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_88b38fda144143079c618225","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tClient-side SSE reconnection — the browser ``EventSource`` state machine\n3\t(bead adc-2vto).\n4\t\n5\tThe companion server-side suite ``tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py`` models\n6\tan SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the *server-side* drain task wrapping\n7\t``broadcaster.event_generator()`` — it proves the *server* still delivers to a\n8\treconnected stream, but it never drives the browser's own ``EventSource``.\n9\t\n10\tThis file is the client-side half. It runs the EXACT inline app script the\n11\tbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\n12\tthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\n13\tmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\n14\tplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\n15\tasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\n16\thalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n17\t\n18\t- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n19\t  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n20\t  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n21\t  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n22\t  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n23\t- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n24\t  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n25\t  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n26\t  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n27\t- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n28\t  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n29\t  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n30\t  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n31\t\n32\tAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\n33\tNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\n34\tthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n35\t\"\"\"\n36\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n37\t\n38\timport json\n39\timport subprocess\n40\tfrom pathlib import Path\n41\tfrom typing import Any\n42\t\n43\timport pytest\n44\t\n45\tfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n46\t\n47\t# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\n48\tES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n49\t\n50\tpytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n51\t    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n52\t)\n53\t\n54\t\n55\t# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n56\t\n57\t\n58\tdef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n59\t    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n60\t\n61\t    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n62\t    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n63\t    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n64\t    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n65\t    \"\"\"\n66\t    if NODE is None:\n67\t        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n68\t    proc = subprocess.run(\n69\t        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n70\t        input=json.dumps(plan),\n71\t        capture_output=True,\n72\t        text=True,\n73\t        timeout=15,\n74\t        check=False,\n75\t    )\n76\t    if proc.returncode != 0:\n77\t        raise RuntimeError(\n78\t            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n79\t        )\n80\t    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tdef _card(\n84\t    label: str = \"Pods\",\n85\t    *,\n86\t    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n87\t    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n88\t    summary: str = \"ok\",\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n90\t    seconds: int = 5,\n91\t) -> dict:\n92\t    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n93\t    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n94\t    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n95\t    return {\n96\t        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n97\t        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n98\t        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n99\t    }\n100\t\n101\t\n102\tdef _plan(\n103\t    *,\n104\t    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n105\t    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n106\t    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n107\t    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n108\t    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n109\t) -> dict:\n110\t    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n111\t    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n112\t    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n113\t    return {\n114\t        \"session_id\": session_id,\n115\t        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n116\t        \"openapi_version\": version,\n117\t        \"cards\": cards or [],\n118\t        \"steps\": steps or [],\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t\n122\tdef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n123\t    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n124\t\n125\t\n126\t# === init + connect ===========================================================\n127\t\n128\t\n129\tclass TestInitAndConnect:\n130\t    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n131\t It runs the EXACT inline app script the\nbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\nthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\nmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\nplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\nasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass TestInitAndConnect:\n    \"\"\"``init()`` registers a surface and opens exactly one ``EventSource``,\n    transitioning the status indicator to \"connecting\" — the baseline a\n    reconnect test starts from.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_init_creates_exactly_one_eventsource(self):\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"initCompleted\"] is True\n        assert t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_eventsource_url_carries_surface_session_and_type(self):\n        \"\"\"The SSE URL the canvas opens carries the registered surface_id, the\n \"session_id=sess-42\" in url\n        assert \"surface_type=canvas\" in url\n\n    def test_status_is_connecting_before_open(self):\n        \"\"\"Before the stream's onopen fires, the dot shows \"connecting\".\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[{\"action\": \"wait\"}]))\n        assert t[\"statuses\"] == [{\"status\": \"connecting\", \"text\": \"Connecting...\"}]\n\n\n# === onopen → loadTopics → connected ==========================================\n\n\nclass TestOnOpenLoadsTopics:\n    \"\"\"``onopen`` flips the status to \"Connected\" and re-fetches ``GET /topics``\n test_initial_cards_render_on_open(self):\n        \"\"\"The card set present at connect time renders into the container.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(\n            cards=[_card(\"Alpha\", test_explicit_close_does_not_spawn_new_eventsource(self):\n        \"\"\"A client-side close() ends the stream without opening a new one.\"\"\"\n        t = run_plan(_plan(steps=[\n reconnect → live event converges ==================\n\n\nclass TestFullLifecycle:\n    \"\"\"The whole sequence a flapping canvas survives: connect, take a transient\n    error, reconnect, then
